title: Postdoc position on AI alignment and safe AI available at EPFL DLAB

titleByLocale.it: Posizione Postdoc sull'allineamento AI e AI sicuro disponibile su EPFL DLAB
titleByLocale.en: Postdoc position on AI alignment and safe AI available at EPFL DLAB
titleByLocale.de: Postdoc-Position zur KI-Ausrichtung und zur sicheren KI bei EPFL DLAB
titleByLocale.fr: Postdoc position on AI alignment and safe AI available at EPFL DLAB

descriptionByLocale.it:
Mission Research topics:
• Alignment
• Large language models
• Natural language processing
• Mechanistic interpretability Applications are invited for a postdoctoral position at EPFL, to be hosted by the Data Science & AI Lab , headed by Prof. Robert West. We seek a candidate who will lead ground-breaking research projects with the goal of building safe AI that truly cares about human interests. Instead of viewing alignment as mere postprocessing of pretrained models (“lipstick-on-a-pig alignment”), you will contribute to our agenda of “raising” models that will be continuously aligned throughout the training process, starting from token 1 in pretraining (see here for a high-level vision). To this end, you will combine synthetic data generation, mechanistic interpretability, LLM pre- and posttraining, and model evaluation in novel ways. Main duties and responsibilities You will contribute to the Swiss AI Initiative , in particular by incorporating your research results into Apertus , SwissAI’s open, transparent, multilingual language model. You will work in a stimulating, open, and international research environment consisting of many highly talented and motivated students, embedded in a strong network of academic and industrial collaborators. Other benefits include a competitive salary (starting at around CHF 88,000 p.a.), an extremely well funded national research system, an office next to a stunning lake and even more stunning mountains, and generous travel support. Involvement in teaching is possible but not required. Project work with MS and PhD students is expected. Profile Candidates should have completed, or be near completion of, a PhD with a strong international publication record in AI or NLP. Strong programming skills and experience with machine learning frameworks are required. We offer
• A stimulating and international working environment
• Competitive salary and excellent working conditions – more information can be found on our website (https://www.epfl.ch/campus/services/human-resources/en/basic-starting-salary-of-doctoral-assistants-and-postdocs/)
• Opportunity to perform state-of-the-art research in one of the most dynamic scientific institutions in Europe.
• Opportunity to interact with internationally renowned experts, and with a strong team of postdoctoral researchers and PhD students EPFL ranks among the world’s top universities in the field of computer science. It is located in Lausanne, Switzerland, a beautiful and vibrant city in an Alpine setting on the shores of scenic Lake Geneva, in the very heart of Europe. English is the main language spoken at EPFL, and Lausanne is highly international, such that no French language skills are required. Informations Only applications submitted through the online platform are considered. You are asked to supply:
• A brief cover letter (pdf, up to 2 pages). And in one PDF:
• A CV with a publication list.
• A research statement (pdf, up to 3 pages).
• Contact details for 3 referees. For any further information, please contact: [XXX] Contract Start Date : 04/01/2026 Activity Rate Min : 100.00 Activity Rate Max : 100.00 Contract Type: CDD Duration: 1 year Reference: 2077

descriptionByLocale.it:
Mission The DIAS laboratory under the supervision of Professor Ailamaki, is seeking an Administrative Assistant to support its financial management, administrative operations, and collaborative activities. Main duties and responsibilities Administrative managemen t
• Staff administration, preparation of hiring/leaving files, employment contracts (academic, non-academic), employment of temporary academic and non-academic staff, visiting PhD students, Teaching assistants, internships (summer@epfl, summer in the lab) etc…
• Organization of trips, meetings, conferences, seminars, symposiums
• Orders and inventories of laboratory materials, equipment
• Creation and follow-up of expense reports and payment orders
• Mail and correspondance in French and English
• Welcoming new team members , reception (staff and visitors)
• Complete travel management for visitors (visiting professors, guests, trainees, visiting students, etc ... ) and invitation file, assistance with visa application, accommodation, reimbursement of expenses
• Absence management
• Agenda management (Professor and Lab)
• Documents and administrative tasks related to teaching Financial management
• Annual Budget preparation
• Follow-up of budget accounts
• Follow-up of the administrative management of research projects or mandates
• Processing of supplier and debtor invoices
• Management of the laboratory's credit card
• Support coordination of collaborations and multi-partner projects
• Assist in grant budget preparation and administrative follow-up
• Track deliverables and reporting requirements Event suppor t
• Provide administrative support for events, workshops, travel, and teaching activities (including invited speakers and summer schools)
• Team building organization Profile
• Experience in budget organization and accounting
• Experience as an administrative assistant and in project coordination
• Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities
• Proactive reliable, and service-oriented, with a “can-do” mindset
• Fluency in French and English required
• Ability to thrive in a multicultural environment and contribute to a dynamic team We offer
• Excellent work conditions
• A multicultural and stimulating environment
• Possibilities of further education Informations Contract Start Date : as soon as possible Activity Rate : 30%-50% Contract Type: CDI Reference: 2203
